pulse: Markets hovering at all-time highs as US-Iran ceasefire talks collapse and Brent crude surges toward $104, while a pivotal CPI print hits at 8:30 AM ET this morning. Trump-Xi Beijing summit on May 14 adds further binary risk to a week already loaded with Fed Chair succession uncertainty as Powell's term expires May 15.

movers.name: BRENT
movers.reason: US-Iran peace talks collapse; Strait of Hormuz traffic near-halt reignites energy disruption fears

movers.name: INTC
movers.reason: Wall Street Journal report of preliminary Apple chip manufacturing agreement lifts Intel

movers.name: GOOGL
movers.reason: Alphabet briefly surpasses Nvidia by market cap; AI stack dominance story accelerates

movers.name: VIX
movers.reason: Geopolitical risk premium builds ahead of CPI print and Trump-Xi summit this week

portfolio: Marc's portfolio faces bifurcated risk this week. US equity exposure (SPX, NDX at ATHs) looks stretched as CPI data lands this morning â€” a hot print could push the Fed further into its 3.5-3.75% hold stance and reprice rate cuts out entirely until 2027, as Bank of America now forecasts. Gold at $4,742 remains a strong store-of-value hedge and continues trending higher; the Iran risk premium is supportive. Oil positions benefit directly from Brent surging toward $104 on Strait of Hormuz fears â€” meaningful short-term tailwind for energy exposure. BTC at $81,762 shows modest gains but sits below the $90K level that would signal renewed institutional momentum. IBEX35 dipped 0.21% on mixed European sentiment. STI above 4,920 shows Singapore resilience. The Trump-Xi summit on May 14 is the biggest binary event â€” a positive outcome could lift Asia including STI; failure could spike VIX and pressure risk assets. EUR/USD at 1.1785 relatively stable but ECB rate hike expectations for June/September could support EUR. FX positioning should stay defensive until CPI and summit outcomes are known.

alerts.text: CPI data release today May 12 at 8:30 AM ET â€” primary market catalyst. Consensus: headline +0.6% MoM / +3.7% YoY, core +0.3% MoM. Hot print = risk-off spike.
US-Iran ceasefire on 'life support' â€” Trump rejected Iran counteroffer May 11. Strait of Hormuz near-halt. Brent at $104. Energy disruption risk elevated.
Fed Chair Powell term expires May 15. Senate confirmation of Kevin Warsh expected this week. Policy uncertainty during transition.
Trump-Xi Beijing Summit May 14-15. Trade delegation talks May 12-13. Binary event for Asia markets and tariff trajectory.
CNMV fined Gerard Pique 200K euros and Jose Elias 100K euros for insider trading in Aspy Global/Atrys Health deal.
Nissan to cut 211 jobs (37% of Catalonia workforce) across El Prat, Zona Franca, and functional centers.

news.title: US-Iran ceasefire on life support as Hormuz traffic halts
news.text: Trump rejected Iran's peace counteroffer on May 11, calling it totally unacceptable. Iran said it will never bow, prolonging the 10-week conflict. Near-halt of Strait of Hormuz traffic â€” normally handling one-fifth of global oil and LNG flows â€” reignites energy inflation fears. Brent surged 4.9% toward $104.

news.title: CPI data lands this morning â€” key macro catalyst
news.text: April US CPI releases at 8:30 AM ET on May 12. Consensus: headline +3.7% YoY, core +2.7% YoY. With Fed holding at 3.50-3.75% and Bank of America forecasting no cuts until H2 2027, a hot print could spike yields and weigh on equities.

news.title: Trump-Xi Beijing summit set for May 14-15
news.text: Chinese vice premier leads trade delegation in Washington May 12-13 ahead of the full summit. Agenda includes trade truce extension, agricultural purchases, AI safeguards, and Taiwan understanding. Markets treating it as the week's biggest geopolitical catalyst.

news.title: S&P 500 hits all-time high despite geopolitical turmoil
news.text: SPX closed at 7,412.84 on May 11, up 0.19%, with Nasdaq also at record levels. Rally driven by chipmakers and AI euphoria. Notable disconnect between record stock prices and unresolved Iran war/oil spike risks.

news.title: Fed holds rates; Powell exits May 15 as Warsh confirmation nears
news.text: Federal Reserve maintained 3.50-3.75% target for third consecutive meeting. April FOMC saw first dissent since 1992 with 8-4 vote. Powell's term expires May 15; Senate expected to confirm Kevin Warsh as successor this week.

news.title: Intel surges 5.7% on Apple chip manufacturing deal report
news.text: Wall Street Journal reported preliminary agreement for Apple to use Intel foundry capacity. Largest single-day move for Intel in months; confirms chipmaker partnership activity accelerating alongside AI infrastructure spending.

news.title: Alphabet briefly surpasses Nvidia by market cap
news.text: Alphabet stock up approximately 160% over the past year, briefly topping Nvidia in after-hours trading on May 10. Growth driven by AI models, distribution, and cloud infrastructure advantage.

news.title: ECB signals June and September rate hikes on Iran energy inflation
news.text: European Central Bank signaling rate increases in June and September 2026 due to Iran war-driven energy price surge. EUR/USD holding at 1.1785 as markets price ECB tightening versus Fed pause divergence.

news.title: USTR initiates second Section 301 tariff review on Chinese goods
news.text: USTR issued notice on May 6 seeking industry comments on 2018 Section 301 tariffs. Effective tariff rates on Chinese goods now exceed 100%, with EV components exceeding 145% cumulative rates. Current reduction agreement extended through November 10, 2026.

news.title: Putin signals openness to Ukraine settlement via European talks
news.text: Russian President Putin announced a settlement possibility for the Ukraine war through European negotiations per SafeAbroad May 11 briefing. No firm proposal made; European diplomatic channels being tested ahead of summer.

news.title: CNMV fines Pique and Elias 300K euros for insider trading
news.text: Spain's CNMV fined former footballer Gerard Pique 200,000 euros and Aspy CEO Jose Elias 100,000 euros on May 11-12 for using privileged information about the Atrys Health acquisition. Both violations classified as very serious under securities regulations.

news.title: Nissan to cut 211 workers â€” 37% of Catalonia workforce
news.text: Nissan announced 211 redundancies across three Catalan facilities: El Prat spare parts plant, Zona Franca NTC center, and functional areas center. Part of broader European restructuring affecting 900-plus jobs. Unions preparing mobilizations.

news.title: Strike at Pascual's Gurb plant over Casa Tarradellas sale
news.text: About 100 workers at Pascual's dairy facility in Gurb struck on May 11 after announcement of plant sale to Casa Tarradellas for mozzarella production. Workers demanding labor guarantees; road blockade at C-17 access roundabout.

news.title: Singapore STI holds above 4,920; Jardine C&C explores asset sale
news.text: Straits Times Index remained resilient above 4,920 through a choppy week, supported by strong US employment data. Jardine C&C reportedly exploring sale of car dealership operations in Malaysia and Singapore; shares up 27% over past 12 months.

news.title: Nebius-Nvidia 2 billion dollar AI cloud partnership announced
news.text: Nebius Group and Nvidia announced a $2 billion AI cloud infrastructure partnership, reflecting continued investment in AI compute capacity outside the US big-tech hyperscaler oligopoly.

day_ahead: Primary catalyst: US April CPI at 8:30 AM ET â€” hot print risks VIX spike and equity selloff; in-line print likely sustains ATH momentum. US-China trade delegation meets in Washington May 12-13 ahead of Trump-Xi Beijing summit May 14-15. 10-year Treasury note auction today. Fed speakers likely cautious ahead of Chair transition (Powell exits May 15). Watch Brent crude for any Hormuz headlines. European session: STOXX50 and IBEX35 open with geopolitical and CPI overhang.

ai_news.title: Anthropic revenue run rate hits 44B, up 80x â€” Google Cloud deal at 200B
ai_news.text: Anthropic's annualized revenue grew 80-fold, surpassing $44 billion run rate. The company committed $200 billion to Google Cloud over five years and signed a SpaceX compute deal. Claude Mythos model focuses on cybersecurity vulnerability identification and is restricted to approved organizations only.

ai_news.title: Pentagon AI deals finalized with OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia â€” excluding Anthropic initially
ai_news.text: Pentagon finalized AI agreements with eight companies on May 4-5, initially excluding Anthropic. White House subsequently reopened discussions with Anthropic following technology breakthroughs. Center for AI Standards and Innovation signed model-testing agreements with Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI on May 5.

ai_news.title: Alphabet 160% rally reflects full AI stack ownership â€” briefly surpasses Nvidia
ai_news.text: Alphabet's stock surge reflects its position across AI models, distribution networks, and cloud infrastructure. Briefly surpassed Nvidia by market cap on May 10, a symbolic milestone in the AI infrastructure race.

ai_news.title: AI-driven corporate restructuring accelerates in May 2026
ai_news.text: Companies across industries are reorganizing around AI capabilities with Chief AI Officers becoming the new power center in Fortune 500 boardrooms. Layoffs in May 2026 reflect function-level automation rather than wholesale headcount cuts, per Yahoo Finance analysis.
